sharedRootCause: A non-public / TIGER road-TYPE tag (road.owner matching /private/) was treated as established private OWNERSHIP. This produced two operator-visible surfaces that called an unknown-ownership road 'private' and asserted recorded private-road rights are required: (1) computeAccessStatus's decision-card summary, and (2) a free-text concern string emitted at the source (public-property-intelligence-live) and carried verbatim through accessStatus.concerns. The first repair fixed only the summary/decision-card path and left the concerns-array string, which is still served in the canonical /report operatorRecord.
whyAutomatedTestsMissedIt: Tests asserted accessStatus.status and (after the first repair) the summary wording, but never asserted the accessStatus.concerns array text, and no test ran the shared sanitizeAccessLanguage over the persisted private-road concern string. The offending text lived in a field no test inspected.
whyBrowserQaMissedIt: The frontend FeasibilityStrip renders only accessStatus.summary + unresolved, not concerns, so the residual is not on the rendered deal card today — only an adversarial reviewer reading the raw /report payload caught it. It is one render/export away from the operator, so it is a real defect in the canonical record.
missingInvariant: No operator-record field (summary, concerns, workStatus, or any evidence string) may assert a road IS private or that recorded private-road rights ARE required from a road-layer/type tag; ownership is 'unknown' until proven, and the recorded-rights requirement is conditional. The shared sanitizeAccessLanguage enforces this on every persisted-run read/write.
missingAcceptanceJourney: Assert the FULL accessStatus object (summary AND concerns) plus the served /report operatorRecord.accessStatus.concerns[] contain no unconditional 'private road' / 'recorded access rights are required' assertion for an unknown-ownership road.
sharedRepair: Extended the shared sanitizeAccessLanguage (which runs at read AND write time on every persisted public-intelligence run) to rewrite 'is mapped as a private road' -> 'carries a non-public/private tag in the road layer, but ownership is unverified' and 'recorded access rights over it are required (and are not confirmed by GIS)' -> 'recorded access rights would be required only if it is confirmed private (unconfirmed by GIS)'. computeAccessStatus already maps every concern through this sanitizer, so all surfaces (summary + concerns) are covered. Also fixed the source guard in public-property-intelligence-live to emit the conditional unknown-ownership wording for new runs.
newRegressionTest: evidence-language.test.ts 'never asserts an unknown-ownership road is private or that recorded rights are required (ws2-r5)' asserts the sanitizer neutralizes the legacy private-road concern; operator-property-record.test.ts asserts computeAccessStatus summary uses unknown-ownership language and de-duplicates concerns.
newBrowserAssertion: On the acceptance card, neither the rendered Road/Access card NOR the served /report operatorRecord.accessStatus.concerns[] asserts the road is private or that recorded private-road rights are required; ownership reads unknown/unverified with a conditional rights clause.
